entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The cereal crop being studied for genetic divergence and resistance to pests.

entities.name: ATHERIGONA SOCCATA
entities.description: The sorghum shoot fly, a pest that affects sorghum crops.

entities.name: RESISTANCE TO SORGHUM SHOOT FLY
entities.description: The ability of sorghum genotypes to resist infestation by Atherigona soccata.

entities.name: SIMPLE SEQUENCE REPEAT
entities.description: Molecular markers used to characterize genetic diversity and link to QTLs.

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE TRAIT LOCI
entities.description: Genomic regions associated with the resistance to sorghum shoot fly.

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A condition used in the development of sorghum hybrids that influences resistance expression.

entities.name: F1 HYBRIDS
entities.description: The first-generation offspring of cross-pollinated sorghum parents.

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The total number of genetic characteristics in the genetic makeup of a species or population.

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The observable physical variations among the sorghum lines and hybrids.

entities.name: POLYMORPHIC INFORMATION CONTENT
entities.description: A measure used to evaluate the informativeness of genetic markers.

entities.name: DISSIMILARITY ANALYSIS
entities.description: The process of analyzing phenotypic and genotypic differences between sorghum lines.
